CONT 	CTS, 	CO 	ISPONDEl\TCE
8c 	CASE 	HISTO 	S
from 	Dan 	Kennedy
for 	Business 	of Copywriting 	Academy
Entire 	Academy© 	2010, 	Dan 	S. 	Kennedy/Kennedy 	Inner 	Circle 	Inc.
All 	Rights 	Reserved
NOTES:
1: THESE DOCUMENT 	SAMPLES 	ARE ENTIRELY CONFIDENTIAL 	AND
PROPRIETARY. 	THEY 	ARE 	NOT 	TO 	BE 	REPRODUCED, 	DISSEMINATED 	OR
EVEN 	DISCUSSED 	OUTSIDE 	YOUR 	OWN 	BUSINESS.
2: 	THESE 	DOCUMENTS 	ARE 	NOT 	PROVIDED 	AS 	TEMPLATES. 	EACH
PROFESSIONAL 	PRACTITIONER 	IE. 	COPYWRITER, 	CONSULTANT, 	ETC. 	IS
ADVISED 	TO 	DEVELOP 	HIS/HER 	OWN 	UNIQUE 	BUSINESS 	DOCUMENTS. 	THE
USE 	OF 	THESE 	SAMPLES 	AS 	EXAMPLES 	FOR 	TEACHING 	PURPOSES 	DOES
NOT 	CONSTITUTE 	LEGAL ADVICE. 	THE 	AUTHOR(S), 	SPEAKER(S),
PROVIDER(S) 	OF 	THIS 	ACADEMY 	ARE 	NOT 	ENGAGED 	IN 	THE 	RENDERING
OF 	LEGAL 	OR 	RELATED ADVICE. IF LEGAL 	OR 	RELATED 	ASSISTANCE 	IS
REQUIRED, THE 	SERVICES 	OF 	A 	COMPETENT, 	APPROPRIATE
PROFESSIONAL 	PERSON 	SHOULD 	BE 	SOUGHT.

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY
Thanks for your fax. Nice to hear from •
agrun.
There are two problems 	re. 	the request: first, timing .... this is 	very 	short notice for
·me; 	every day allocated for consulting through the entire first quarter 	of 	06 	has already
been assigned to clients, and finding a day soon enough to be of help with an April event
is nearly impossible. There's absolutely nothing available in either January or February,
so the only option would be to violate my holiday vacation and use either December 	27,
28 	or 	29. 	I'm 	sure that's 	not 	a desirable option 	f 	you and Tonja. It's not for
me ei 	er, 	and, 	frankly, the only reason 	I'd 	do it would be 	some 	assistance/opportunities
with 	er and above the fee for the day.
Second, 	I 	have a client exclusivity issue 	in 	the dental 	industr~, 	and woul 	need to
be certain Dr. 	Paul 	Searby had no objection to my consulting on this matter 	wi
The fee for the day is 	$9;700.00. 	In this instance, 	I'd 	want two additional
concessions, one minor, one significant. First, 	I 	have four new books hitting bookstores
in February and March, and would want to promote the books and related free
teleseminars via 	an 	insert in 	sletter and fax or e-mail to his list, with his
endorsement, at no charge. 	Of 	greater significance - but also an opportunity - 	I 	am
working with a partner on a new, niched marketing system for dentists targeting a unique
type of patient, to be licensed to one dentist per geographic area. Coincidentally, we're
launching in April. Having 	my 	dentist partner speak at th 	vent and promote the
program there 	is 	very attractive .... this would provide income 	well. We
could work out details in same day 	of 	consulting on the seminar.
I 	do want to mention, 	I'll 	have no time to take on any copywriting assignments for
the 	vent; we will be limited to what 	we 	accomplish during the day together. I can
assemble a number 	of 	seminar/boot camp marketing examples from other clients to show
you ... .I 	assume, amongst other things, you are looking for ideas and strategies to boost
attendance, and 	in 	many instances, 	I've 	increased on-going clients 	by 	as 	much as double
03 	to 	04, 	and 	04 	to 	05. 	If 	you want to talk about optimizing on-site 	revenues- 	pretty
good 	at 	that. 	(Our 	own most recent event had 	450 	in 	attendance; 2.7-million in revenue.)
So, 	if you want to take December 	27, 28 	or 	29 	on this basis, let me know
immediately; I'll then query 	Paul 	and confirm with you as quickly as possible. My 	office
closes December 	21 	-January 	5, and Vicky and I will both be 	100% 	incommunicado, so
we 	do need 	to resolve 	this quickly. 	.

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY

Thanks for your fax, and congratulations on your business life transformation.
I agree: the product described has enonnous potential. Coincidentally, I imagine you
noticed reporting in last 	week's 	news about the damage people are doing to their 	teeth
using the off-shelf whitening products, so having one that improves dental 	healt;h
would capitalize on that reporting. 	You 	may or may not know, I did a lot of work on
the 	tooth whitening infomercial hosted by Vanna White, and do a lot 	of 	advertising
work 	for cosmetic dentists and cosmetic surgeons.
Unfortunately, I 	am 	extremely restricted in 	my 	ability 	to 	accept new clients and
projects now and in foreseeable future. I have a 	27 	member 	Private 	Client Group
paying access fees to be assured 	of 	my availability, and with very rare exception, am
now turning down everything else except the occasional situation referred by a client,
and providing both very substantial front fees as well as upside potential. I book
months out, by the way, not weeks, and have a waiting list for consulting days for 	2nd
half this calendar year. I tell you this so you do not view me as rude; obviously, I
appreciate the consideration, as well as your GKIC membership.
With that said, I would be interested in freeing up time only 	if 	you were committing a
sizeable budget, 	for 	full-page national magazine print, inserts, TV infomercial, direct-
mail, and online marketing 	($1,000,000.00 	to 	$1,500,000.00 	minimum); then I would
consult on all 	of 	it, write copy 	for 	some 	of 	it, 	etc. per an agreed upon plan, with
compensation 	of 	fees and royalties. 	An 	initial fee 	of 	$50,000.00 	would be required, to
arrange a one day consultation and development 	of 	the complete battle plan, fee fully
credited to subsequent back-end royalties. And you should anticipate a total fee 	of
three to four times that. 	If 	that fits, then I could find/liberate a day 	to 	meet sometime
in 	March, with work beginning in April, at earliest .... Vicky know, and she'll get a
telephone appointment scheduled for us on 	my 	next available telephone day.
Incidentally, this is the kind 	of 	project requiring involvement 	of 	others in 	"my 	world"
with expertise, contacts, etc. in different specialties. 	The 	one area you mentioned in
which I claim no expertise whatsoever is retail distribution. However, not mentioned
are opportunities for catalog placements; sale 	of 	a pro 	or 	private label-version
to/through dentists and/or beauty shops and/or other 	similar 	businesses; and private
label deal 	with 	a network marketing company. -
Alternately, there is a waiting list for consulting days 	2nd 	half this year at 	my 	regular
rate 	of 	$9,700.00 	per day (not credited against project fees 	or 	royalties), and there
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 44 --

•
•
may 	be 	days available to new clients after 	Private 	Group clients get first choices.
Vicky 	can 	add 	you 	to this list and inform 	you 	when 	dates are released.
As 	a 	third 	alternative, 	I'm 	happy 	to compile a referral list for 	you 	of 	different
C~JlSultants, 	copywriters, infomercial producers, etc. 	who 	could assist 	you 	with
different 	pieces. 	There 	is 	no 	one 	identical to 	me 	to 	suggest. 	The 	fee for this would
merely 	be 	an 	hour 	of 	my 	time, billed same as 	phone 	consulting 	(only 	available 	to 	on-
going 	clients) at 	$800.00.
•
As 	you 	know, 	my 	fax# 	is: 602-269-3113 .

DANS. 	KENNEDY
Thanks 	for 	your follow-up fax.
I also thought 	we 	had 	a 	good, productive day, and would be 	open- 	schedule permitting,
when 	the 	time 	comes- 	to 	working with you guys. 	As I 	told you, the economics are 	.
challenging 	for 	any and 	all 	direct-response, but 	I 	believe 	there's 	sufficient potential for
success 	to 	experiment 	in 	different niches, with different media and different approaches.
I 	am 	cautious 	and 	selective, 	for 	two 	reasons: one, the royalties are more important than
the 	fees; 	and 	two, I 	don't 	like blemishes 	on 	my 	track record 	if 	avoidable. 	Success 	is not
just 	about the product, market and 	math- 	it's 	also about the people .... do they 	"get 	it"?,
can 	they implement?, 	will 	they 	work?, etc., 	and 	it's 	pretty obvious you guys qualify.
So, 	as 	you 	rnake 	some 	decisions, try this 	and 	that, 	keep 	me 	posted.
Re. 	your 	web 	site work: 	I 	can 	tell 	you 	who 	to 	study: 	Matt 	Furey (mattfurey.com), John
Alanis (wmenapproachyou.com), 	and 	us. 	Recommending someone to actually do the site
work 	is dicier. 	The 	few champs I know are either captive 	(like 	our 	guy)-or 	booked for
months 	and 	extremely pricey. 	I'm 	going to put 	out 	a few feelers this week and try and
get 	a few options to 	you 	late next week.
Best, •
Dan

Greg 	Renker, King 	Of 	All He Can 	See
Greg,
Four quickies ....
. 	. 	He 	and 	nice 	guys, 	smart about
a number 	of 	things, in love with and married 	to 	a pretty goo 	and saleable product that
lends itself 	to 	a 	lot 	of 	niched print and direct-mail .... but they are burdened with 	bad
economics .	and (the typical) delusional inventor. 	I 	did what 	I 	could in a day, mostly
clarifying their realities and options. 	I 	do 	not 	believe they were ready 	to 	immediately go
forward with print and direct-mail 	and 	copywriting 	for 	same, even though 	they'd 	'
indicated 	they 	were when obtaining the 	day 	with 	me- 	but 	I 	am 	over-booked 	and 	over-
committed anyway, so 	I 	actually considered that a blessing. 	We 	may 	or 	may 	not 	get
together 	at 	a 	later date. 	I 	believe they were satisfied 	with 	value received. Just thought
you'd 	like a report.
Two, 	John 	Volturo 	(without 	Elliott) 	attended 	the 	recent 	SuperConference, 	got 	a

-- 12 of 44 --

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY
4-12 -06
•
Re. Critique
Roberta, •
The 	letter 	too 	quickly leaps into product ( 	~ 	I'd 	advise a longer build-up,
dealing with the 	day 	to 	day pressures and frustrations 	of 	trying 	to 	wear many hats,
capitalize 	on 	all opportunities, get great plans prepared without working nights and
weekends 	to 	dismay 	of 	spouse and kids - 	in 	short, emotional matters, agitating problems.
Also, 	in marketing 	to 	attorneys, everyone but another attorney 	is 	at 	great disadvantage.
I'd 	either re-write the whole pitch 	in 	the voice 	of 	a 	champion 	lawyer, sent on 	his
stationery, lawyer 	to 	lawyer 	OR 	use a lift note 	on 	lawyer's 	letterhead 	to 	push reading
everything 	OR 	at 	bare 	minimum, 	work 	a strong 	lawyer 	testimonial into first page.
The 	headline is horrid, except for tease 	of 	free offer. 	Decide 	what the main fear 	or 	benefit
promise is, and 	go 	with it. 	This 	isn't 	it, but 	as 	example:
FREE 	To The First 92 Grossly Over-Worked Attorneys Who Respond:
Stop Burning The Midnight 	Oil 	Grinding 	Out 	Trusts, 	Partnerships 	And
Estate Plans With 	Your 	Old 	Cut 	'N 	Paste 	Patchwork 	Quilt....
The 	Choate testimonial is 	okay 	and is useful 	somewhere, 	but 	it 	merely authenticates the
product. 	What 	you 	really 	need are 	testimonials 	from 	lawyers about 	money 	made, 	time
saved, personal/family life re-gained, being 	at 	kids' ball games, breaking 	free 	of 	boredom,
burn-out 	and 	drudgery.
The 	monthly 	calls 	are 	pitched 	very 	poorly. 	This 	should 	be 	a big 	deal, 	and 	made 	into a 	big
value.
The 	offer 	and pricing, more 	complicated than 	need 	be. 	I'd 	recommend 	switching 	to 	a
simple, 	set 	monthly 	charge with 	first three 	months 	free, 	than 	auto-charge. 	Make 	it 	about
the 	membership with the 	uct and upgrades 	included; 	not installment
payments 	for 	the thing. No first year 	vs. 	second year differential, 	just 	equalize 	and 	be
done with 	it. •
Other 	than the offer/pricing, 	all 	the 	copy 	that's here 	is 	okay, 	clear, 	but 	dull.
•
You're 	right about adding (at least) an audio CD, too. ..
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

my 	telephone appointments; 	other days are dedicated to other 	purposes. 	S
coming 	back 	with lists 	of days and times choices 	doesn't 	really work. 	I'm 	done 	now 	for
December, and simply 	can't 	find 	room 	for 	an 	exception- 	sorry. 	Next 	days 	Vicky 	has 	to
work 	with 	is January 18 	and 	February 22 .... although 	you 	do 	have 	an 	already 	scheduled
Platinum 	call 	in 	January.
If, 	in 	interim, 	there's 	something I might 	get 	and 	respond 	to 	by 	fax, 	I'll 	do 	my 	best.
Sorry 	about this.
Dan

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY
2-27-10
Mike
Mike:
Per 	our 	call. ...
1.) : 	Congratulations 	on 	the opportunity at 	hand. 	Under 	sep 	cover> 	I'm 	getting 	an 	in-depth
research report 	on 	the Christian market sent 	to 	you, from a research firm 	I 	use 	as a source
for various clients 	and 	projects.
- -·.
• 	- 	- - 	1	2): 	Vicky 	will 	be 	getting 	you 	contact info for 	, who
operate 	the 	business 	I 	spoke 	with 	you 	about. 	I 	believe there could
be 	big 	(secular) opportunity there - their 	media 	platform, 	reach 	to 	and 	trust relationship
with 	parents is 	big 	and 	growing. For a 	brief time, they 	are 	going 	to 	do revenue-share
arrangements 	with 	entrepreneurs, 	in 	advance 	of being able 	to 	sign 	more 	traditional
sponsorship deals with big, brand-name companies. 	If .you 	get 	in 	now, 	you 	are likely 	get
to 	stay 	in. 	And, 	you 	may 	be 	able to assist 	them 	in 	some reciprocal way, maybe reach into
martial arts 	world- 	for example, they have the ability 	to 	package and private label free
bowling 	as 	a premium for distribution 	by 	others.
3): 	If you 	have serious interest 	in 	having 	me 	work 	in-depth 	on 	one 	or 	more 	projects
including copywriting, I suggest a meeting while 	I 	am 	in 	Dallas 	for 	the 	Glazer-Kennedy
SuperConference- 	work 	it 	out 	with Vicky. 	In 	interim 	or 	otherwise, 	I'm 	happy 	to 	provide
the 	telephone consulting. Note: check 	was 	made 	out 	to 	"DKIC"- 	I'll 	endorse 	it 	over, 	but
in 	future, please: 	KENNEDY 	INNER 	CIRCLE, INC. 	~
Best,
Dan
PS: 	A 	few 	random 	thoughts 	I 	didn't 	get 	to 	in 	call:
Re. book: 	I 	suggest 	integrating 	testimonials/human interest-success stories that illustrate
and 	prove 	each 	idea 	and 	strategy, 	throughout the book. 	At 	end, it needs a summary 	and
clear 	blueprint, do this 	Day 	I, 	this 	Day 	2, etc. 	Don't 	be 	subtle 	about pushing other,
specific resources 	you 	offer 	throughout 	the 	book 	and/or driving 	to 	a 	web 	site to see
videos, find resources, etc. - 	do 	not rely merely 	on 	promotion 	at 	back 	of book. (Most
never 	get 	there!).
Re.: 	testimonials 	absent 	full 	name 	and ideally 	info 	ie. 	occupation, married/divorced,# 	of
kids, etc. 	lack 	believability. 	If some 	must 	be 	'names 	withheld 	on 	request', 	many 	MUST
bear 	full 	identification. 	As 	I said, I 	suggest 	grouping 	by 	problem.
-

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY
1-11-10
Dr.
I was very impressed with the practice launch strategy and results 	you
described during the year-end call (and, for whatever 	it's 	worth, I think 	you
should have won).
As 	you 	probably know, 	I've 	done a great deal 	of work 	in the professions,
with private practice marketing; and 	in 	marketing to the professions, since
the late 1970's. 	Your 	ability to generate a lot 	of new 	patients 	in 	a 	short-term
promotion is highly prized, and seems 	to 	me 	to have application 	to
chiropractic as well as dentistry, 	and 	to 	"re-launches" not 	just 	new 	practices.
Potentially, to certain other types 	of businesses as welL 	I 	have, 	in 	fact,
called 	your 	interview to 	the 	specific attention 	of two 	of my 	clients: Dr. Chris
Tomshack, 	CEO 	ofHealthSource 	(which has conversion-franchised 	over
200 	chiropractic practices 	in 	3 years); 	and 	Chris 	Hum 	re. 	the 	Kennedys
Barber Clubs.
I rarely "prospect" for clients 	or 	hunt 	for opportunity, and I have no idea
what 	your 	plans 	or 	interests are in leveraging it, by opening your 	own
practices (only); 	by 	franchising; 	by 	speaking, training, coaching; 	etc.- 	but 	if
I 	might 	be 	of assistance, 	I 	would 	be 	interested. 	If you 	would like 	to 	talk, let
Vicky at 	my 	Phoenix office know, 	and 	she'll 	arrange a telephone
appointment for us. (Ph. 	602-997-7707, 	fax 602-269-3113.) 	Ifnot, 	that's
fine 	and 	no 	response necessary; 	we're 	both busy.
Best,
Dan 	Kennedy

Thanks 	for 	a 	great day. 	It's 	a pleasure working with you.
This 	will confirm our agreement re. the copywriting/consulting project I 	am 	completing
for 	your company. 	· 	·
M 	~~ 	....!..!...!:E~l.!...
and 	Kennedy: 	In.,er 	Circl~, 	Inc. 	('-er~inaft~r: 	CONSULT~NTl
PROJECT 	DESCRIPTION: 	Advertising/marketing 	campaign 	for 	Client's business
opportunity 	('' 	· 	Consultant") 	as 	described 	in 	its 	current literature.
Copywriting for:
A) 	Lead 	Generation 	ads, fullpg., 1/2 	pg., 	1/3rd 	pg. , 	classifieds
B) 	Recorded 	Message Script
C) Main Follow-Up 	Pkg. 	Including sales letter, 	enclosures
D) 	2 	to 	3 	follow-up 	letters 	(w. 	offer 	changes) 	including 	a 	TeleSeminar
•
campatgn
E) 	Addit'l 	forTeleSeminar 	campaign: FAX, 	e-mail, voice broadcast
F) 	Re-write letter 	sent 	with contract+ enclosures
Above 	will 	be done in 3 versions: (a) generic, (b) 	financial 	services niche (accountant,
CPA), 	(c) 	insurance 	sales niche. •
Consultant will 	also 	provide samples 	of 	and/or 	access 	to 	several 	different Tele-Sem.inars
as models for 	Client.
CONTINUING 	AQYICE 	& 	SERVICES: 	Consultant shall be 	available 	for 	telephone
consulting 	regarding 	this project while the work 	is 	being 	completed, and 	subsequently 	as
long 	as Client is 	testing 	and/or using 	work 	and 	royalties are being paid, under a
"reasonable 	man 	standard" 	meaning 	that 	such time and 	access 	must be 	limited 	to
essential discussion. 	Client 	also 	understands 	that 	Consultant is rarely available on short
notice, and messages 	or 	FAXes 	may 	be answered after several days, and telephone
conferences 	may 	require advance scheduling. 	Should 	additional in-person meetings be
required 	by 	Client, Consultant's published hourly or daily 	fees 	will 	apply. 	Also, 	as 	long
as Client is testing/using this work product and 	paying 	royalties, Consultant will modify,
revise, 	'.'tweak" and 	seek 	to improve 	copy 	as 	warranted 	by 	results. 	However, 	should a 	,
•
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 44 --

complete rewrite 	or 	a 	new 	project 	be 	required, a separate agreement 	and 	separate
compensation 	will be 	required.
COMPENS.Arf.ION 	TO 	CONSULTANT: 	The 	fee 	for 	the entire project, based 	on
Consultant's 	standard 	daily rate, is 	$70,200.00, 	less a 	10% 	courtesy 	discount 	for 	size 	of
project; 	net 	fee 	$63, 	180.00. 	Credit 	for 	initial 	day's 	consultation fee 	paid 	of 	$7,800.00•
leaves 	a 	fee 	balance 	of $55,380.00. 	Fee 	balance 	is 	to 	be 	paid 	in 	four 	installments:
$13,845.00 	to 	initiate work; 	$13,845.00 	in 	December 	within 	10 	days 	of 	Consultant's
delivery 	of 	rough 	drafts; 	$13,845.00 	in 	January 	2003 	within 	10 	days 	of 	Consultant's
deli very 	of final drafts; 	and 	$13,845.00 within 	30 	days 	of 	Consultant's 	delivery 	of final
drafts. •
In 	addition to the 	fee, 	a royalty is to be paid 	on 	gross revenues 	from 44
Units 	of 	sale" 	ie.
"consultant 	wholesale 	purchase 	agreements" 	in 	excess 	of 30 	per 	month, 	of 2% 	of 	such
gross revenues, 	for 	as 	long 	as 	work 	product 	provide4 	by 	Consultant 	is 	used 	in 	whole 	or
substantial 	part, 	with 	or 	without 	nlddificauon:-'~ 	-~~
Royalties are 	to 	be 	accounted 	for 	and 	paid 	monthly, 	by 	the 	30th 	of 	the 	current 	month 	for
the 	revenues 	of 	the 	prior 	month. Consultant reserves right, with 	reasonable 	advance
notice, 	to 	inspect 	Client's 	books and records 	including 	but 	not 	limited 	to 	merchant
account 	documents, 	bank 	statements 	and 	shipping records, 	to 	ascertain accuracy 	of
royalty 	payments.
All 	fees 	and 	royalties 	per 	this 	agreement are 	to 	be 	paid 	to 	Kennedy 	Inner 	Circle 	Inc.,
5818 	N. 	7th 	str. 	#103, 	Phoenix, 	Arizona 	85014 	unless 	Client 	is 	otherwise 	notified 	in
writing.
INDEMNITY: 	It 	should 	be 	noted 	that 	Client 	accepts full 	and 	sole responsibility 	for 	the
accuracy 	and 	legality 	of 	the 	copy as 	it 	is 	used 	in 	final 	fonn 	and 	for 	whatever 	use 	Client
makes 	of 	it 	in 	advertising, direct-mail, etc., 	and 	indemnifies 	Consultant 	against 	any 	all
liabilities 	or 	costs 	arising 	from 	Client's 	use 	of 	provided 	copy 	and 	advice. 	Both 	parties•
acknowledge 	that 	Consultant 	cannot 	be 	expected 	to 	be 	familiar 	with 	the 	legal 	aspects 	of
Client's 	business, 	and 	should 	Client 	need 	independent 	legal 	evaluation 	of furnished 	copy
or 	advice, 	Client 	will 	seek 	such 	counsel 	from 	a 	lawyer 	or 	other 	appropriate 	professional.
OWNERSHIP 	OF 	WORK: 	All 	work 	performed 	and 	provided 	by 	Consultant 	is 	'work for
hire', 	and 	product 	of 	that 	work 	is 	the 	property 	of 	Client, 	and 	may 	be 	copyright 	protected
by 	Client, 	with 	the 	caveat 	and 	acknowledgement 	that 	there 	may 	be 	recycling 	of certain
ideas, 	themes 	or 	copy 	in 	this 	work for 	this 	Client 	and 	other 	past 	or future 	work 	by
Consultant for other 	Clients. 	Consultant 	reserves 	right to use work for example purposes
in 	Consultant's 	seminars, 	coaching 	programs 	and 	publications 	such 	as 	books 	and
newsletters .
•
TIMETABLE: 	Target 	date 	for 	rough 	drafts 	is 	December 	16, 	2002. 	Target 	date 	for 	fmal
drafts 	is 	January 	15, 	2003. 	Every 	effort 	will 	be 	made 	by 	Consultant 	to 	meet 	or 	beat 	these
target 	dates, 	however 	variance 	of 	up 	to 	15 	days 	is 	permitted 	without 	constituting 	breach
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 44 --

of 	contract. Delivery 	of 	final drafts is contingent upon prompt feedback and cooperation
of 	Client, and Consultant is not responsible for additional 	delays 	that 	may 	be caused 	by
Client.
If 	this 	accurately summarizes 	our 	agreement, please date, 	sign and 	return 	a 	copy 	along

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY
2-22-10
Thanks 	for 	fax 2-16. Glad 	to 	hear 	of your interest.
•
To 	be 	a 	bit 	clearer, 	what 	I would suggest I assemble is a large 	"bank" 	of blocks 	of copy,
organized 	by purpose ie. headlines, opening paragraphs, product benefits, guarantee, etc.
as 	well 	as 	by 	tactic ie. agitate embarrassment, tie 	to 	upcoming life event .... which 	can 	be
drawn 	from 	piece-meal and used 	by 	anybody working 	on 	I'd 	also like to deliver
a 	few 	complete 	FSI's, 	direct-mail pieces, trying 	both 	different copy 	and 	different formats.
The 	"bank" 	will 	need 	to 	be fee only; the complete pieces would ordinarily 	be 	fee+royalty,
but 	given 	size and scope 	of total project 	istorical aversion 	to 	open-end
obligation, 	I'm 	willing 	to 	set the entire thing 	up 	based 	on 	fee only, 	or 	fee divided, 	with
part 	paid 	only based 	on 	continued use after 	a 	test 	tenn.
If I 	do 	this, 	I 	want 	to 	do 	it 	right, 	with 	no scrimping 	or 	comer-cutting, so it will require
substantial 	time 	commitment 	on 	my part, 	and 	I'll 	even 	pay 	some 	"readers" I trust 	to
critique 	the 	copy 	before 	I 	do 	final drafts. 	I'd 	like 	to 	put 	the equivalent 	of 	10 	to 	15 days
into this.
I 	think 	we 	should have a 	brief phone conversation, 	and 	go 	from 	there. 	I'll 	have Vicky set
up 	the appointment.
Best,
Dan

DAN 	S. 	KENNEDY
AGREEMENT 	BETW ~EEN: 	lnc.(hereinafter: 	CLIENT) 	and
KENNEDY 	INNER 	CIRCLE 	INC. 	(hereinafter: 	CONSULTANT)
PROJECT 	DESCRIPTION:
(1): 	Preparation 	of 	an extensive 	"bank" 	of 	direct-response copy for use 	in 	all 	media,
including but not limited to, print advertisements, insert media, direct-mail, online, etc.,
seJiing 	and products; copy including headlines, opening paragraphs, body
copy, stories and testimonials, offers and calls to action, bonus offers, guarantees; copy
for and organized by various purposes - ie. embarrassment tied to different situations
; 	all organized and cross-referenced so it can be used
in 	"Chinese 	menu 	fashion" 	by Client's staff and freelance copywriters. Majority 	of
"bank" 	will 	be 	directed at current, primary customers ie 	· 	but
also smaller 	"banks" 	for 	(2): 	At 	least 3 to 5 complete
ads/FSI's and/or direct-mail pieces. (Note: 	all 	copy provided to Client for unlimited use.)
Copy may 	be furn ish ed with rough layout guidelines but NOT in printer ready 	fonn. Copy provided both in hard copy and 	diskette, 	Word, Mac fonnat,
with no warranty of compatibility. 	Providers 	Of Services: 	All work will 	be personally done or supervised by DanS. Kennedy, 	President, Kennedy
Inner Circle, 	Inc . 	Under his supervision, 	a project team 	including freelance 	contributors, writers and 	consultants may 	be used.
CONTINUING 	ADVICE 	& 	ADDITIONAL 	SERVICES:
(1) 	Consultant shall 	be 	available 	for 	telephone 	consulting 	regarding 	this 	project while 	the 	work 	is
being 	completed, 	under 	a "reasonable 	man 	standard" 	meaning 	that 	such 	time 	and 	access 	must 	be
limited 	to 	essential 	discussion. 	Client 	also 	understands 	that 	Consultant 	is 	rarely 	available 	on 	short
notice, 	and 	messages 	or 	FAXes 	may 	be 	answered 	after 	several 	days, 	and 	telephone 	conferences
require 	advance 	scheduling. 	(2) 	Should 	additional 	in-person 	meetings 	be 	required 	by 	Client
Consultant's 	published 	hourly 	or 	daily 	fees 	may 	apply. 	(3)Vendor 	recommendations 	may 	be 	made
by 	Consultant 	to 	Client 	involving 	vendors 	in 	which 	Consultant 	has 	ownership 	or 	interest or 	from
which 	Consultant receives 	fees, 	commissions 	or 	other 	compensation. 	Such 	details 	will 	not be
disclosed.
COMPENSATION 	TO 	CONSULTANT:
Fee: 	, 	to be paid 	$/o,OOO.OO 	at 	contract, 	~,000.00 	upon review 	of
preliminary, partial Work (suffi 	·ent to provide indication 	of Work 	itself and
extensiveness 	of project), and 	,000.00 	upon 	delivery 	of finished product (within 	15
days 	of delivery 	or 	prior to first use 	of any 	of the Work). 	Customary royalties are waived
as courtesy based 	on 	long relationship with Client. Any fees not paid as agreed cause
cease 	of all work and Client's use 	of already completed work. 	No 	invoices are tendered;
agreed upon payment is Client's responsibility. Note: this fee is substantially discounted
from Consultant's current fees and therefore must 	be 	kept completely confidential. Any
disclosures Consultant may make 	of its 	own 	accord concerning fee 	or 	financial
arrangements 	of this contract to third parties shall not constitute waiver 	of requirement 	of
confidentiality on Client. (*Client may terminate project 	at 	this point, with 	no 	obligation
beyond the initial 	,000.00 	payment, providing none 	of the Work is used.)
All 	fees 	per 	this 	agreement are 	to 	be 	paid 	to 	Kennedy 	Inner 	Circle 	Inc., 	5818 	N. 	7th 	Str. 	#103, 	·
Phoenix, 	Arizona 	85014 	unless 	Client 	is 	otherwise 	notified 	in 	writing. 	Payments 	per 	this 	agreement
are 	assignable 	by 	Consultant.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 44 --

INDEMNITY:
It 	should 	be 	noted 	that 	Client 	accepts 	full 	and 	sole 	responsibility 	for 	the 	accuracy 	and 	legality 	of 	the
copy 	as 	it 	is 	used 	in 	final 	form 	and 	for 	whatever 	use 	Client 	makes 	of 	it 	in 	advertising, 	direct-mail,
etc., 	and 	indemnifies 	Consultant 	against 	any 	and 	all 	liabilities 	or 	costs 	arising 	from 	Clienfs 	use 	of
provided 	copy 	and 	advice. 	Both 	parties 	acknowledge 	that 	Consultant 	cannot 	be 	expected 	to 	be
familiar 	with 	the 	legal 	aspects 	of Client's 	business, 	nor 	is 	Consultant 	expected 	to 	be 	expert 	in
applicable 	federal 	and/or 	state 	laws, 	and 	should 	Client 	need 	independent 	legal 	evaluation 	of
furnished 	copy 	or 	marketing 	advice, 	Client 	will 	seek such 	counsel 	from 	a lawyer 	or 	other
appropriate 	professional. 	Consultant 	specifically 	claims 	no 	expertise 	in 	either 	the 	specific
advertising, 	marketing 	or 	sales 	aspects 	of Client's 	business 	or 	the 	legal 	aspects 	of Client's
business. 	Client 	is 	specifically 	cautioned 	that 	there 	are 	established 	and 	(as 	of 	late 	2009) 	new
Federal 	Trade 	Commission 	rules 	and 	regulations 	governing 	advertising 	claims, 	testimonials 	and
celebrity 	testimonials, 	as 	well 	as 	other 	federal 	and 	states' 	laws 	governing 	advertising; 	it 	is 	the
Client's 	sole 	responsibility 	to 	determine 	the 	compliance 	of its 	advertising, 	marketing 	and 	sales
content 	and 	practices . Consultant 	will 	modify 	the 	Work 	as 	needed 	to 	comply 	with 	Client's
interpretations 	of said 	regulations 	at 	Client's 	request., 	at 	any 	time 	during 	the 	term 	of 	this
agreement. 	Under 	no 	circumstances 	can 	Consultant 	be 	held 	liable 	in 	any 	way, 	for 	any 	reason, 	for
an 	amount 	exceeding 	compensation 	paid 	to 	Consultant 	by 	Client. 	Should 	Consultant 	be 	named 	in
any 	regulatory 	agency 	or 	other 	legal 	actions 	filed 	against 	Client, 	Client 	warrants 	it shall 	exert 	all
possible 	efforts 	to 	have 	Consultant 	severed 	and 	released 	from 	such 	actions, 	co-operate 	with
Consultant's 	separate 	counsel's 	efforts 	to 	effect 	such 	severance, 	and 	bear 	or 	reimburse
Consultant's 	legal 	costs 	incurred 	in 	effecting 	such 	severance 	and/or 	in 	defending 	against 	the
action, 	with 	Consultant 	retaining 	right 	of 	choice 	of 	its 	counsel.
OWNERSHIP 	OF 	WORK: .
All 	work 	performed 	and 	provided 	by 	Consultant 	is 	'work 	for 	hire', 	and 	product 	of 	that 	work 	is 	the
property 	of 	Client, 	and 	may 	be 	copyright 	protected 	by 	Client, 	with 	the 	caveat 	and 	acknowledgement
that 	there 	may 	be 	recycling 	of certain 	ideas, 	themes 	or 	copy 	in 	this 	work 	for 	this 	Client 	and 	other
past 	or 	future 	work 	by 	Consultant 	for 	other 	Clients. 	Consultant 	reserves 	right 	to 	use 	work 	for
example 	purposes 	in 	Consultant's 	seminars, 	coaching 	programs 	and 	publications 	such 	as 	books
and 	newsletters. 	Consultant 	reserves 	right 	to 	publish 	and 	distribute 	copies 	of 	the 	copywriting 	work
performed 	per 	this 	agreement 	with 	members 	of 	his 	Look 	Over 	Shoulder 	Program, 	Platinum 	Inner
Circle, 	No 	B.S. 	Jnfo-Markettng 	Letter, 	No 	B.S. 	Marketing 	Letter 	and/or 	No 	B.S. 	Marketing 	To 	The
Affluent 	Letter 	subscribers 	and/or 	as 	samples 	in 	manuals 	used 	in 	seminars 	or 	products. 	RE-SALE
RIGHTS 	RESTRICTION: 	Under 	no 	circumstances 	is 	Client 	authorized 	to 	create 	an 	information
product 	for 	sale 	that 	is 	comprised 	entirely 	or 	primarily 	of 	the 	copy 	provided 	by 	Consultant 	and/or
Client's 	marketing 	materials 	utilizing 	said 	copy, 	nor 	is 	Client 	authorized 	to 	otherwise 	re-sell 	said
copy 	via 	licensing, 	publication, 	shared 	use 	or 	other 	means 	or 	media. 	Copy 	is 	provided 	only 	and
exclusively 	for 	use 	by 	Client 	in 	Client's 	own 	business, 	for 	advertising, 	marketing 	and 	promotion 	of
the 	product(s)/service(s) 	described 	in 	this 	agreement.
TIMETABLE:.
Preliminary examples 	of the 	Work 	as 	described above to 	be 	provided to Client by 	May 	31
or before. Final Work to 	be 	provided 	by 	July 	19 	or before.
-
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

•
Meeting 	these 	targets 	is 	dependent 	upon 	prompt 	responses 	by 	Client 	to 	Consultant's 	request for
information, 	material , and 	copy 	approvals. 	While 	every 	attempt 	is 	made 	to 	avoid 	delays 	and 	effect
on-time 	or, 	when 	possible, 	early 	deliveries, 	Consultant 	cannot 	be 	held 	liable 	for 	missed 	target
dates.
TERM: .
Agreement 	is 	in effect from date 	of 	initiation below to 	120 	days following delivery 	of
final product (with regard to modifications requested 	by 	Client). Client use 	of Work
described herein has no expiration 	or 	renewal dates.
If 	this 	accurately 	summarizes 	our 	agreement, please date, sign 	and 	return 	a copy,
with payment/initial payment.

Thought 	you'd 	be interested 	in 	this 	-I've 	got my "reader team" in place for this project
(which I do for most major 	projects- 	but 	it's 	a quasi-secret). 	I pay 	'em 	to 	read, 	not
write; comment on, critique all 	my 	drafts, long before 	you 	see them. The team 	I've 	put
together for this includes:
•Woman: Freelance copywriter, 	Has 	written for a player 	in
· 	who 	is 	a Glazer-Kennedy Member.
•Woman: In-house copywriter for 	Freelances on side. Articles published
products
•Woman: Freelance writer 	fo 	. Blog 	fo
•Woman: 	Young, 	junior copy editor at a 	"hip" 	agency. Clients incl
•
or 	g1g:
•Guy: 	Direct sales vet turned copywriter. Sold
•
they'll 	raise
interesting questions and arguments, know the 	better 	than
I, 	know the 	tribal language, so 	I find 	running my 	work 	through a group like this helps.
I also have assembled a 	half dozen un-paid interns, 	with 	less 	pro 	experience, 	but 	one 	way
or 	another, 	in 	tune with the market and students 	of direct-response. 	They'll 	be 	working 	to
learn not earn. 	I 	also have one other Member 	who'll 	do critiques for 	me 	on 	this: 	he 	runs a
. 	in 	Canada, and 	is 	a very aggressive marketer.
Finally, 	I'll 	get critique from Sydney 	Barrows- I 	co-authored a book with 	her 	you
probably 	haven't 	seen, so sending 	. She did 	a 	4-year 	project·
• 	•
good instincts.
That's 	my behind the scenes ''reading 	team" 	for 	your 	work. 	I'm 	slowly getting organized,
shortly to get to work 	in 	earnest. -
Best,

advertising. 	ffitimately, 	if I 	have 	a 	secret 	room 	full 	of incredibly 	gifted

references, 	anecdotes 	and 	exan1ples 	have 	appeared 	over 	the 	years 	in 	my

Thank 	you 	for your fax.
I recall Gary speaking 	of your company, and I imagine the direct-mail piece
is in 	my 	swipe-file archives. 	Gary 	was 	a good friend. 	And- 	I have often
said- 	pound 	for pound, arguably the best copywriter, when he could be
made to focus and work. 	On 	three occasions, I, and others, tried beating his
controls and no one could. 	I 	do have 	a 	control 	he 	tried to beat and couldn't,
though, so 	that's 	something!
As 	you 	probably know, 	I 	have done and do a fair amount 	of work·
· 	(although 	my 	#I 	specialty is with
'information products'). Coincidentally, last 	week 	just 	before 	your 	inquiry,
and 	a 	team 	of six from 	flew into Cleveland for 	a
strategy day with 	me 	re.
I 	am certainly open 	to 	working 	with 	you. 	To 	be 	clear, 	I 	do 	not 	operate as a
copywriter given assignments; rather, as a strategic marketing advisor, 	who
often (but 	not 	always) also writes copy. 	My 	experience includes print,
direct-mail, 	TV 	infomercials 	and 	other media, as well as working with
countless celebrities and navigating the waters 	of celebrity endorsements.
All 	new 	client relationships begin with a day 	of initial exploration,
discussion 	of strategies, "diagnosis 	and 	prescription", 	and 	picking 	of my
brain, fee 	$18,800.00. 	Guaranteed, 	of course; should 	you 	judge 	the 	day
unproductive, happy 	to 	refund 	your 	fee. 	Beyond 	that, most 	work 	is
fee+royalty 	by 	project, sometimes 	with 	an 	umbrella monthly retainer
depending 	on 	level 	of consulting activity. 	I 	am 	very 	rarely 	just 	preparing 	an
ad 	or 	a 	mailing, instead working 	on 	multi-media, multi-step projects, 	with
typical fees from 	$50,000.00 	to 	$100,000.00, 	occasionally 	much 	higher.
One 	thing: you 	may 	have 	assumed 	I'm 	in 	Phoenix. I 	am 	not. 	Only 	my
assistant, 	Vicky 	is. 	I 	am 	in 	Ohio 	90% 	of the 	time, another 	home 	in 	Virginia
most 	of the other time, rarely travel on biz and almost 	never west 	by 	design,
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Sorry, but I 	don't 	do the work 	if I 	can't 	show it 	off and teach from it; bad use 	of my time.
Everybody 	signs the same contract. And I have long relationships with most 	of 	my
clients.
Transcript: paper, please. 	Same 	with calls: 	if I am to review them productively, they
need transcribed and provided in printed form. May 	tum 	out your Barbados problem
won't 	be 	script related but cultural. 	If people have negative attitudes about selling, best
script in the world gets "magically" sabotaged.
Your 	quick 	'n 	dirty welcome letter is (a) feel good; (b) re-sell, so the main points 	of ad+
call, making them feel 	they've 	made a good, smart decision .... erasing most common
negatives, 	buyer's 	remorse, which I 	haven't 	even begun working 	on 	myself, but 	I'd 	hope
you'd 	know. Immediately occurs to me: 	price- 	have I (foolishly) paid too much? Doubts
- does this really work? Will 	it 	work for me?
Dan
